fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Missouri Physical Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a physical therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Missouri - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: physical therapist license reciprocity missouri, how to transfer physical therapist license to missouri, physical therapist compact missouri, missouri physical therapist endorsement application

board.name: Missouri Board of Healing Arts — PT
board.mailing_address: 3605 Missouri Boulevard, P.O. Box 4, Jefferson City, MO 65102

license_types.title: PT, DPT
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a physical therapist in Missouri.

steps.title: Register and Apply Online
steps.description: Create an account and complete the initial application for Physical Therapist licensure through the Missouri Division of Professional Registration Online Application System.

steps.title: Submit Official Transcripts
steps.description: Arrange for official transcripts, verifying your physical therapy education and degree, to be sent directly from your educational institution to the Missouri Board of Healing Arts.

steps.title: Complete Fingerprinting and Background Check
steps.description: Follow the Board's instructions to schedule and complete fingerprinting for a criminal background check through the state-approved vendor.

steps.title: Pass the Missouri Jurisprudence Examination
steps.description: Successfully complete the Missouri Physical Therapy Jurisprudence Examination, typically accessible online through the Board's system or a designated vendor.

steps.title: Pass the National Physical Therapy Examination (NPTE)
steps.description: Register with the Federation of State Boards of Physical Therapy (FSBPT) and achieve a passing score on the National Physical Therapy Examination (NPTE).

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination).

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NPTE. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Missouri physical therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: For endorsement applicants, the state licensing fee is $80. Additional third-party costs (fingerprinting, transcripts, or verification) may apply.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination) is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Missouri have reciprocity for physical therapists?
faqs.answer: Yes. Missouri participates in the PT Compact. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Missouri without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my physical therapist license to Missouri?
faqs.answer: Current board processing time is 4 to 6 Weeks once your file is complete.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Missouri while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Physical Therapist in Missouri
hero.dek: To apply for a Physical Therapist license in Missouri, prospective applicants must use the Missouri Division of Professional Registration Online Application System to submit their application and required documentation.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal
variants.description: How to renew your existing credential

official_links.portal_name: Missouri Division of Professional Registration Online Application System
official_links.registration_steps:
1. Navigate to the Online Application System login page.
2. Click the 'Register' button if you are a new applicant.
3. Follow the prompts to create a user account, providing necessary personal details and creating a username and password.
4. Log in with your new credentials to start a new application for physical therapist licensure.
